runs.content: Fair Isaac (FICO) picked up at the previous close ($1,227.34) — still ~39% below Morningstar's $2,020 fair value on a wide-moat, dominant credit-scoring franchise, with no earnings until Jul 29 avoiding the binary risk other watchlist names carry this week. No exits today: all 25 legacy positions repriced with no new stop-loss, T1, or T2 triggers, though chip-adjacent names extended gains on the continuing sector rebound (AMAT +23.22% with T1+T2 already banked, TNET +32.03% the largest open gain).

AMD picked up at the previous close ($503.57) as the clearest catalyst-rich leader of the chip-sector rebound (SOX +3% Jul 20) after last week's semiconductor bear market — confirmed Microsoft/OpenAI/Meta/Oracle AI demand, three analyst price-target hikes this week, and the dated Advancing AI 2026 conference (Jul 22–23) landing while the position is open. Three positions (CAT, IQV, IBM) hit the 60-day max-hold limit on the same day and closed out (+0.05%, +17.18%, +7.2% blended respectively — IQV was the portfolio's first closed win); LRCX was stopped out at −18.16% on the lingering chip selloff.
